charge_explanation.policy: Use invoice data to explain normal billing changes before implying any remediation. This bucket covers proration, seat increases, usage overages, annual renewals, and tax being displayed separately.

credit_or_waiver_review.policy: Use account and invoice evidence to determine whether the customer may qualify for a credit, waiver, or manual review. This bucket covers duplicate charges, late-fee waivers, setup-fee waivers, and outage or SLA service credits.

refund_status.policy: Use this when the customer asks about the status of an already-approved refund. Share the current refund status and next milestone without claiming the refund has completed unless the data says so.

invoice_update_request.policy: Use this when the customer needs invoice metadata corrected, such as a PO number or remit-to detail. Explain that invoice updates require human billing operations review rather than claiming the invoice was changed.

contract_or_cancellation_review.policy: Use this when the charge may conflict with cancellation timing or signed commercial terms. Explain what the invoice shows, avoid guessing at contract outcome, and offer human review when the customer disputes timing or contract alignment.
